title: Godot Test Game
description: Developed a simple 2D game using the Godot Engine, focusing on core gameplay mechanics, character movement, and basic collision detection. This project allowed me to explore game development concepts, including scene management, scripting in GDScript, and implementing user input handling.

title: Joule Thief Circuit
description: Designed and built a step-up voltage converter circuit (Joule Thief) capable of powering a standard 220V AC lamp using only a low-voltage 1.5V to 3V DC battery. This project demonstrates practical electronics engineering skills in voltage conversion, circuit design, and maximizing energy efficiency from small power sources.

title: Gas Detector System
description: Designed and built a functional gas detection system using an Arduino Nano, an MQ-135 gas sensor, a 16x2 LCD display, and LED indicators for real-time air quality monitoring. Developed to fulfill the final project requirements for the Microcontroller 1 course, this project served as a major milestone where I practically applied and learned Arduino programming (C/C++) and Assembly language for the very first time.

title: Water level analog and digital sensor
description: As the Project Manager, I directed the overall planning and execution of a dual-method water level monitoring system, coordinating closely with my teammate who handled the technical implementation. The project aimed to create a reliable monitoring tool using two distinct sensing methods: a digital HC-SR04 ultrasonic sensor for precise water height measurement, and an analog transistor-based sensor circuit to indicate discrete water status (low, medium, and high). I oversaw the workflow and ensured the seamless integration of both hardware systems into a centralized, real-time monitoring dashboard visualized through LabVIEW software.

title: Strairs climbing robot
description: As the Head of the Electrical Team, I led the electrical design and integration for a stair-climbing robot engineered with a unique impact-driven climbing mechanism, allowing it to autonomously ascend by strategically bumping into steps. My primary responsibilities centered on the complete hardware wiring and power management. I successfully wired the six 12V 25GA370 DC motors to the ON/OFF relay modules, which acted as the main controllers driven by an ESP32. To ensure reliable and safe power delivery during the high-torque climbing process, I also custom-assembled a 2S2P battery pack fully integrated with a Battery Management System (BMS) for optimal voltage regulation and battery protection.

title: PID controller Water Level system
description: In this project, a Proportional-Integral-Derivative (PID) control system for water level management was developed as an educational module using Arduino Uno. As the PCB Designer, I was responsible for the end-to-end electronic hardware realization, starting from the schematic creation and PCB layout routing using EasyEDA software. The custom board was designed to integrate key components, including an Arduino Nano for VL6180X LiDAR sensor data acquisition, LM2596 step-down modules for power distribution, and an XY-MOS driver for water pump actuation. Furthermore, I executed the manual hardware fabrication process, which involved layout transfer, Ferric Chloride (FeCl3) etching, precision drilling, and component soldering. This custom circuitry successfully bridged the sensor reading module with the main PID controller, ensuring stable real-time data transmission and accurate actuator control.

title: AC electricity Smart meter
description: Engineered an end-to-end AC power profiling tool integrating hardware and software. Programmed a Raspberry Pi Pico in MicroPython to acquire real-time electrical parameters (V, A, W, Wh) via Modbus RTU. Developed a local hardware interface with configurable over-power cutoff protection, and built a desktop GUI application using Tkinter and Matplotlib for live serial data streaming, graphical analysis, and data export.
